title: Socket RAPL Feature Validation at SOC Integration Level
subsection content: This test plan targets the post-silicon validation of Socket RAPL functionality at the highest feature hierarchy, focusing on its integration and operation within the SOC. Socket RAPL provides power and frequency limit enforcement across multiple domains (core, memory, etc.), interfacing with BIOS, TPMI, and various IPs. Key flows include BIOS initialization and locking of RAPL limits, TPMI-based domain enumeration and access, propagation of power/frequency ceilings from root to leaf IMHs and CBB dies, aggregation and real-time reporting of performance status, and coordinated enforcement via cross-domain PID controllers. The plan covers interactions between BIOS, TPMI, IMHs, CBB dies, and system-level status reporting, ensuring robust enforcement and accurate system state reflection under all operational and stress scenarios.

title: Socket RAPL Feature Validation at SOC Integration Level
subsection content: The scope encompasses validation of BIOS and TPMI configuration integrity (including programming, locking, and domain enumeration), propagation and enforcement of power/frequency limits across all IPs, aggregation and reporting of LEAF_PERF_STATUS and RAPL_PERF_STATUS, stress and boundary testing (including negative scenarios and error handling), and cross-domain PID controller interactions. All flows from initialization to runtime enforcement, status reporting, and system response to invalid or extreme configurations are included. Out-of-scope items are any features not directly related to Socket RAPL, such as non-RAPL power management, unrelated BIOS/TPMI features, or IPs outside the RAPL domain.

title: Socket RAPL Feature Validation at SOC Integration Level
Socket RAPL Feature Validation at SOC Integration Level
subsection content: All features require access to BIOS configuration interfaces, TPMI registers, CSR access for RAPL limits and lock bits, and system-level monitoring tools for status registers. Hardware must support domain enumeration and real-time status reporting. Stress tools for generating extreme power/frequency demands and error injection capabilities are needed.

title: Socket RAPL Feature Validation at SOC Integration Level
subsection content: Potential security threats include unauthorized modification of RAPL limits post-lock, improper domain enumeration exposing sensitive configuration, and manipulation of status reporting. Mitigations involve strict enforcement of lock bits, access control on TPMI and CSR interfaces, and validation of error handling for invalid configuration attempts.

title: Socket RAPL Feature Validation at SOC Integration Level
subsection content: Risks include incomplete coverage of cross-domain interactions, undetected failures in lock enforcement, and inaccurate status aggregation under stress. Contingencies involve additional targeted testing for edge cases, enhanced monitoring during stress scenarios, and fallback to manual validation if automated tools fail.

title: Socket RAPL Feature Validation at SOC Integration Level
subsection content: Prerequisites include a fully functional BIOS with RAPL programming capability, TPMI register access, SOC hardware supporting all RAPL domains, and availability of stress and error injection tools. Initial domain mapping and lock bit configuration must be completed prior to test execution.

title: Status Aggregation & Reporting
subsection content: Validate aggregation and reporting of LEAF_PERF_STATUS and RAPL_PERF_STATUS across all domains. Trigger stress events, boundary conditions, and throttling, then confirm that status registers accurately reflect which IPs are limited and the system's response. Expected outcome: status registers are updated in real time and correctly represent system state.

title: Stress-Induced Throttling: Real-Time Update of LEAF_PERF_STATUS and RAPL_PERF_STATUS Across Domains
subsection content: Validate real-time updates and aggregation of LEAF_PERF_STATUS and RAPL_PERF_STATUS counters during stress-induced power excursions across RAPL domains.

title: Stress-Induced Throttling: Real-Time Update of LEAF_PERF_STATUS and RAPL_PERF_STATUS Across Domains
subsection content: Configure RAPL limits near expected workload, apply randomized high-power stress to cores/memory, and sample status registers at <1ms intervals to observe real-time counter increments and aggregation.

title: Stress-Induced Throttling: Real-Time Update of LEAF_PERF_STATUS and RAPL_PERF_STATUS Across Domains
subsection content: Randomly select subsets of cores and memory channels, vary number and location of stressed elements per iteration to maximize coverage.

title: Stress-Induced Throttling: Real-Time Update of LEAF_PERF_STATUS and RAPL_PERF_STATUS Across Domains
subsection content: All RAPL domains enabled and configured via CSR/TPMI, CBBs and IMHs operational, platform default settings, logging infrastructure for HPM messages and status registers.

title: Stress-Induced Throttling: Real-Time Update of LEAF_PERF_STATUS and RAPL_PERF_STATUS Across Domains
subsection content: Involves CBBs, IMHs, RAPL domains (Socket, Platform, DRAM), and HPM message aggregation; counters are aggregated from CBBs to IMHs and up to IMH-P.

title: Stress-Induced Throttling: Real-Time Update of LEAF_PERF_STATUS and RAPL_PERF_STATUS Across Domains
subsection content: Explicit logging of LEAF_PERF_STATUS and RAPL_PERF_STATUS fields, observability of aggregation, and verification of no missed or spurious increments.

title: Real-Time Counter Update Under Randomized Stress
subsection content: Configure all RAPL domains with power limits near expected workload consumption using CSR/TPMI. Randomly select a subset of cores and/or memory channels and apply high-power workloads to exceed the set limits. Continuously sample LEAF_PERF_STATUS HPM messages from CBBs to IMHs and RAPL_PERF_STATUS registers at IMH-P and IMH-S at intervals less than 1ms. Explicitly log the relevant fields for each domain. Remove stress and observe counter behavior.

title: Real-Time Counter Update Under Randomized Stress
subsection content: 1. Boot system with all RAPL domains enabled and configured via CSR/TPMI. 2. Set Socket, Platform, and DRAM RAPL power limits near expected workload. 3. Randomly select cores/memory channels and apply high-power workloads to trigger power excursions. 4. Sample and log LEAF_PERF_STATUS and RAPL_PERF_STATUS at <1ms intervals during stress. 5. Remove stress and continue sampling. 6. Repeat with different random subsets and locations.

title: Real-Time Counter Update Under Randomized Stress
subsection content: Pass if LEAF_PERF_STATUS and RAPL_PERF_STATUS counters increment in real time for affected domains during stress, aggregate correctly to IMH-P, and stop incrementing when stress is removed. Fail if there are missed, spurious, or delayed increments, or incorrect aggregation.

title: Real-Time Counter Update Under Randomized Stress
subsection content: Review logs of LEAF_PERF_STATUS and RAPL_PERF_STATUS fields for real-time, accurate, and location-correct increments. Confirm aggregation matches pseudocode.

title: Real-Time Counter Update Under Randomized Stress
subsection content: Enable SST, Central, and Concurrency checkers. Enable PVT checkers for RAPL domain aggregation and real-time update.

title: Real-Time Counter Update Under Randomized Stress
subsection content: Tool and Content Requirements: Use OSBV/SVOS with supported version, PMON for power/performance monitoring, PythonSV for register access, Supercollider for workload generation, and logging tools for HPM messages.

title: Real-Time Counter Update Under Randomized Stress
subsection content: Test Requirements: BIOS with RAPL support enabled, default OS kernel and scheduler, up-to-date PCIe/storage drivers, and any required registry keys for power management.

title: Real-Time Counter Update Under Randomized Stress
subsection title: Test HW Requirements and HW Configuration *
subsection content: Platform with all RAPL domains, CBBs, and IMHs operational. SoC stepping as per validation plan, DIMMs and PCIe devices populated, thermal solution as per platform default, debug instrumentation for HPM message capture.

title: Aggregation Consistency Across CBBs and IMHs
subsection content: With all RAPL domains enabled and configured, apply stress to different combinations of CBBs and IMHs. Sample and log LEAF_PERF_STATUS and RAPL_PERF_STATUS at <1ms intervals. Verify that aggregation from CBBs to IMHs and up to IMH-P matches the documented pseudocode and that counters reflect the number and location of limited IPs.

title: Aggregation Consistency Across CBBs and IMHs
subsection content: 1. Boot system with all RAPL domains enabled. 2. Set power limits via CSR/TPMI. 3. Apply stress to selected CBBs/IMHs. 4. Sample and log status registers and HPM messages at <1ms intervals. 5. Compare aggregation results to expected values per pseudocode. 6. Repeat with different CBB/IMH combinations.

title: Aggregation Consistency Across CBBs and IMHs
subsection content: Pass if aggregation is correct and counters reflect the number/location of limited IPs. Fail if aggregation is incorrect or counters do not match expected values.

title: Aggregation Consistency Across CBBs and IMHs
subsection content: Analyze logs for correct aggregation and real-time updates. Cross-check with aggregation pseudocode.

title: Aggregation Consistency Across CBBs and IMHs
subsection content: Enable SST, Central, and Concurrency checkers. Enable PVT checkers for aggregation logic.

title: Aggregation Consistency Across CBBs and IMHs
subsection content: Tool and Content Requirements: OSBV/SVOS, PMON, PythonSV, Supercollider, logging tools.

title: Aggregation Consistency Across CBBs and IMHs
subsection content: Test Requirements: BIOS with RAPL support, OS kernel and drivers as per platform default.

title: Aggregation Consistency Across CBBs and IMHs

subsection content: Platform with all CBBs and IMHs operational, SoC stepping as per plan, DIMMs and PCIe devices, thermal solution, debug instrumentation.

title: Stress Removal and Counter Stability
subsection content: After applying stress and observing counter increments, remove all stress workloads and continue sampling LEAF_PERF_STATUS and RAPL_PERF_STATUS at <1ms intervals. Verify that counters stop incrementing immediately and remain stable.

title: Stress Removal and Counter Stability
subsection content: 1. Apply stress as in previous TCs and observe counter increments. 2. Remove all stress workloads. 3. Continue sampling and logging status registers and HPM messages. 4. Verify counters stop incrementing and remain stable.

title: Stress Removal and Counter Stability
subsection content: Pass if counters stop incrementing immediately after stress removal and remain stable. Fail if counters continue to increment or show spurious changes.

title: Stress Removal and Counter Stability
subsection content: Review logs for counter stability post-stress removal.

title: Stress Removal and Counter Stability
subsection content: Enable SST, Central, and Concurrency checkers.

subsection content: Tool and Content Requirements: OSBV/SVOS, PMON, PythonSV, Supercollider, logging tools.

title: Boundary Condition and Throttling Priority: Single and Multiple Domain Limiting
subsection content: Validate correct priority and 1-hot flagging in RAPL_PERF_LIMIT and accurate counter increments for single and multiple domain limiting scenarios.

title: Boundary Condition and Throttling Priority: Single and Multiple Domain Limiting
subsection content: Configure RAPL limits to isolate single domain limiting, then overlap limits for multiple domains. Randomly select limiting domains and apply stress to trigger limiting. Sample and log RAPL_PERF_LIMIT and LEAF_PERF_STATUS.

title: Boundary Condition and Throttling Priority: Single and Multiple Domain Limiting
subsection content: Randomly select which domain's limit is set lowest per iteration; randomize stress application to trigger single or multiple domain limiting.

title: Boundary Condition and Throttling Priority: Single and Multiple Domain Limiting
subsection content: All RAPL domains enabled, BIOS and OS support for RAPL, logging infrastructure for HPM messages and status registers.

title: Boundary Condition and Throttling Priority: Single and Multiple Domain Limiting
subsection content: Involves RAPL domains, CBBs, IMHs, and HPM message reporting; priority logic ensures only one flag is set in RAPL_PERF_LIMIT.

title: Boundary Condition and Throttling Priority: Single and Multiple Domain Limiting
subsection content: Explicit sampling and logging of RAPL_PERF_LIMIT and LEAF_PERF_STATUS, verification of 1-hot flagging and priority order.

title: Single Domain Limiting and 1-Hot Flag Verification
subsection content: Configure power limits so that only one RAPL domain is limiting at a time. For each iteration, randomly select which domain is most constraining and apply workloads to exceed that limit. Sample RAPL_PERF_LIMIT HPM message and LEAF_PERF_STATUS counters. Verify that only one flag is set in RAPL_PERF_LIMIT and only the corresponding counter increments.

title: Single Domain Limiting and 1-Hot Flag Verification
subsection content: 1. Boot system with all RAPL domains enabled. 2. Set power limits so only one domain is limiting. 3. Randomly select limiting domain and apply stress to exceed its limit. 4. Sample and log RAPL_PERF_LIMIT and LEAF_PERF_STATUS. 5. Repeat for each domain.

title: Single Domain Limiting and 1-Hot Flag Verification
subsection content: Pass if only one flag is set in RAPL_PERF_LIMIT (1-hot) and only the corresponding LEAF_PERF_STATUS counter increments. Fail if multiple flags are set or counters increment incorrectly.

title: Single Domain Limiting and 1-Hot Flag Verification
subsection content: Review logs for 1-hot flagging and correct counter increments.

title: Negative Test: Interface Lockout and Feature Disable
subsection content: Validate that interface lockout and feature disable prevent changes to RAPL_PERF_STATUS, LEAF_PERF_STATUS, and RAPL_PERF_LIMIT, ensuring no aggregation or reporting occurs for locked/disabled domains.

title: Negative Test: Interface Lockout and Feature Disable
subsection content: Lock CSR or TPMI interface or disable RAPL feature, attempt to modify power limits, and monitor status registers for any change while keeping power consumption constant.

title: Negative Test: Interface Lockout and Feature Disable
subsection content: Randomly select domains and interfaces to lock or disable per iteration.

title: Negative Test: Interface Lockout and Feature Disable
subsection content: All RAPL domains enabled, ability to set VR_INTERFACE_LOCKED and PBM_DISABLE, logging infrastructure for HPM messages and status registers.

title: Negative Test: Interface Lockout and Feature Disable
subsection content: Involves RAPL domains, CSR/TPMI interfaces, and aggregation logic; lockout and disable must block status updates.

title: Negative Test: Interface Lockout and Feature Disable
subsection content: Explicit logging and verification that status registers remain unchanged during lockout/disable scenarios.

title: Negative Test: Interface Lockout and Feature Disable
subsection content: Attempt to modify power limits on locked/disabled interfaces and verify no status change.

title: CSR Interface Lockout Verification
subsection content: With all RAPL domains enabled, lock the CSR interface for a selected domain by setting VR_INTERFACE_LOCKED. Attempt to modify the power limit for the locked domain while keeping power consumption constant. Monitor RAPL_PERF_STATUS, LEAF_PERF_STATUS, and RAPL_PERF_LIMIT for any change.

title: CSR Interface Lockout Verification
subsection content: 1. Boot system with all RAPL domains enabled. 2. Set VR_INTERFACE_LOCKED for a selected domain. 3. Attempt to modify power limit for the locked domain. 4. Keep power consumption constant. 5. Sample and log RAPL_PERF_STATUS, LEAF_PERF_STATUS, and RAPL_PERF_LIMIT. 6. Repeat for other domains.

title: CSR Interface Lockout Verification
subsection content: Pass if no change is observed in RAPL_PERF_STATUS, LEAF_PERF_STATUS, or RAPL_PERF_LIMIT for the locked domain. Fail if any status register changes.

title: CSR Interface Lockout Verification
subsection content: Review logs for absence of status changes during interface lockout.

title: Real-Time Update and Aggregation Timing: Sampling and Propagation Latency
subsection content: Validate that LEAF_PERF_STATUS and RAPL_PERF_STATUS are updated in real time and reflect stress events within the documented slowloop/HPM message interval.

title: Real-Time Update and Aggregation Timing: Sampling and Propagation Latency
subsection content: Configure RAPL limits, apply and remove stress workloads at random intervals, and sample status registers at high frequency to measure latency between event and counter update.

title: Real-Time Update and Aggregation Timing: Sampling and Propagation Latency
subsection content: Randomly select subsets of cores/memory channels and randomize stress event timing (sub-millisecond to several milliseconds).

title: Real-Time Update and Aggregation Timing: Sampling and Propagation Latency
subsection content: All RAPL domains enabled, high-frequency sampling infrastructure, logging tools for HPM messages and status registers.

title: Real-Time Update and Aggregation Timing: Sampling and Propagation Latency
subsection content: Involves RAPL domains, CBBs, IMHs, and HPM message propagation; aggregation and update timing are validated.

subsection content: Explicit measurement of latency between stress event and counter update, verification against slowloop interval.

title: Stress Event Onset to Counter Update Latency
subsection content: Configure RAPL limits to allow rapid triggering of stress events. Randomly apply stress to subsets of cores/memory channels at sub-millisecond to several millisecond intervals. Sample LEAF_PERF_STATUS and RAPL_PERF_STATUS at intervals less than 1ms. Measure and log the time between stress onset and the first observed increment in status counters.

title: Stress Event Onset to Counter Update Latency
subsection content: 1. Boot system with all RAPL domains enabled. 2. Set power limits for rapid stress triggering. 3. Randomly select cores/memory channels and apply stress at random intervals. 4. Sample and log status registers at <1ms intervals. 5. Measure latency from stress onset to counter increment. 6. Repeat for multiple iterations.

title: Stress Event Onset to Counter Update Latency
subsection content: Pass if latency is within the documented slowloop/HPM message interval (<1ms). Fail if latency exceeds this interval or increments are missed.

subsection content: During repeated application and removal of stress at random intervals, monitor for any missed or delayed increments in LEAF_PERF_STATUS and RAPL_PERF_STATUS. Compare event timing to counter updates to detect any lag or missed events.

subsection content: Involves RAPL domains, CBBs, IMHs, and HPM message reporting; priority logic ensures only one flag is set, but counters increment for all limited domains.

title: Cross-Domain Stress: Simultaneous Limiting and Aggregation Consistency
subsection content: Explicit logging and verification of flagging and counter increments for all affected domains during simultaneous limiting.

title: Simultaneous Domain Limiting and Flag Consistency
subsection content: Configure power limits so that simultaneous stress on multiple RAPL domains is possible. Randomly apply high-power workloads to both cores and memory channels to exceed limits for multiple domains. Sample RAPL_PERF_LIMIT and LEAF_PERF_STATUS. Verify that only the highest priority domain is flagged in RAPL_PERF_LIMIT, but all affected LEAF_PERF_STATUS counters increment.

title: Simultaneous Domain Limiting and Flag Consistency
subsection content: 1. Boot system with all RAPL domains enabled. 2. Set power limits for simultaneous limiting. 3. Randomly apply stress to cores and memory channels. 4. Sample and log RAPL_PERF_LIMIT and LEAF_PERF_STATUS. 5. Verify flagging and counter increments. 6. Repeat with different stress combinations.

title: Simultaneous Domain Limiting and Flag Consistency
subsection content: Pass if only the highest priority flag is set in RAPL_PERF_LIMIT and all affected LEAF_PERF_STATUS counters increment. Fail if multiple flags are set or counters do not increment as expected.

title: Simultaneous Domain Limiting and Flag Consistency
subsection content: Review logs for correct flagging and counter increments during simultaneous limiting.

title: Simultaneous Domain Limiting and Flag Consistency
subsection content: Enable SST, Central, and Concurrency checkers. Enable PVT checkers for aggregation logic.

title: Simultaneous Domain Limiting and Flag Consistency
subsection content: Tool and Content Requirements: OSBV/SVOS, PMON, PythonSV, Supercollider, logging tools.

title: Simultaneous Domain Limiting and Flag Consistency
subsection content: Test Requirements: BIOS with RAPL support, OS kernel and drivers as per platform default.

title: Simultaneous Domain Limiting and Flag Consistency

subsection content: Platform with all RAPL domains, CBBs, and IMHs operational, SoC stepping as per plan, DIMMs and PCIe devices, thermal solution, debug instrumentation.

title: Aggregation Rule Verification for Multiple Domains
subsection content: During simultaneous limiting, verify that LEAF_PERF_STATUS counters for all affected domains increment according to aggregation rules, and that RAPL_PERF_LIMIT reflects only the highest priority limiter. Compare observed increments to expected values based on stress application.

title: Aggregation Rule Verification for Multiple Domains
subsection content: 1. Apply simultaneous stress as in previous TC. 2. Sample and log LEAF_PERF_STATUS and RAPL_PERF_LIMIT. 3. Compare counter increments to expected values per aggregation rules. 4. Verify only one flag is set in RAPL_PERF_LIMIT.

title: Aggregation Rule Verification for Multiple Domains
subsection content: Pass if counters increment as per aggregation rules and only one flag is set. Fail if aggregation is incorrect or multiple flags are set.

title: Aggregation Rule Verification for Multiple Domains
subsection content: Analyze logs for aggregation consistency and correct flagging.

title: Aggregation Rule Verification for Multiple Domains
subsection content: Enable SST, Central, and Concurrency checkers.

subsection content: Tool and Content Requirements: OSBV/SVOS, PMON, PythonSV, Supercollider, logging tools.

subsection content: Test Requirements: BIOS with RAPL support, OS kernel and drivers as per platform default.

title: Stress Removal and Counter Stability in Multi-Domain Scenario
subsection content: After simultaneous limiting and counter increments, remove stress from all domains. Sample LEAF_PERF_STATUS and RAPL_PERF_LIMIT to verify that counters stop incrementing and all flags are cleared.

title: Stress Removal and Counter Stability in Multi-Domain Scenario
subsection content: 1. Apply simultaneous stress and observe counter increments. 2. Remove all stress workloads. 3. Sample and log LEAF_PERF_STATUS and RAPL_PERF_LIMIT. 4. Verify counters stop incrementing and flags are cleared.

title: Stress Removal and Counter Stability in Multi-Domain Scenario
subsection content: Pass if counters stop incrementing and all flags are cleared after stress removal. Fail if counters continue to increment or flags remain set.

title: Stress Removal and Counter Stability in Multi-Domain Scenario
subsection content: Review logs for counter stability and flag clearing post-stress.

subsection content: Enable SST, Central, and Concurrency checkers.
